fact: passport-saml (@node-saml/passport-saml) validates the IdP certificate at strategy construction time via assertRequired. Passing a stub cert (e.g. 'TODO_CONFIGURE') causes an immediate throw. In integration tests override via overrideProvider(SamlStrategy).useValue({ validate: () => null }) to bypass construction entirely.
why: The strategy constructor calls assertRequired() before any request is processed, so no test ever reaches the validate() method if the cert is invalid.

fact: NestJS Test.createTestingModule().overrideProvider(token) only works when the token is already registered as a provider in the module under test. Injection tokens (e.g. 'MAILER_SERVICE') must appear in the module's providers array before they can be overridden in tests.
why: overrideProvider replaces an existing DI registration; if the token is absent NestJS has nothing to replace and the override is silently ignored or throws.

fact: nestjs-pino activates the pino-pretty transport when NODE_ENV !== 'production'. If pino-pretty is not installed, the integration test suite crashes at NestApplication init. Set NODE_ENV=production in the test:e2e script to disable pretty transport without adding pino-pretty as a devDependency.
why: pino-pretty is optional for development logging; making it required in tests adds a runtime dependency that serves no testing purpose.

fact: Store refresh tokens using a {tokenId}:{rawToken} cookie format where tokenId is the DB row UUID and rawToken is a 64-byte hex random value. At validation: fetch by tokenId (O(1) indexed lookup), then bcrypt.compare(rawToken, storedHash). This avoids full-table scans while keeping tokens unguessable.
why: Hashing the token before storage prevents exposure if the DB is compromised; using a UUID prefix avoids scanning all rows to find which token matches.

fact: Prisma rejects Record<string, unknown> for JSON column inputs (InputJsonValue incompatibility). Use the parameter type extracted directly from the create/upsert signature: `as Parameters<typeof this.prisma.model.create>[0]['data']['field']` to get the exact expected type without using `any`.
why: Prisma's InputJsonValue excludes undefined and requires literal JSON types; Record<string, unknown> is too broad and triggers a TS error at the assignment.

fact: Anonymous arrow functions returned from makeWrapper() helpers trigger the react/display-name ESLint rule. Use named function declarations instead: `function Wrapper({ children }) { return ...; }; return Wrapper;` inside the factory.
why: ESLint's react/display-name rule requires all React components to have an identifiable name for React DevTools. Arrow functions have no inferred name when returned from another function.

fact: The regex /sign in/i used with getByRole('button') matches both 'Sign In' and 'Sign In with SSO', causing a 'Found multiple elements' error. Use an anchored regex /^sign in$/i for exact label matching when multiple buttons share a prefix.
why: Testing Library's accessible name matching is substring-based by default; anchoring the regex to start/end prevents partial matches on longer button labels.

fact: Add src/main.tsx to coverage.exclude in vite.config.ts. The React entry point (createRoot + StrictMode) contains no business logic and cannot be meaningfully unit tested, so it should not count against coverage thresholds.
why: Including main.tsx in coverage pulls the function % down because createRoot's callback is never called in the jsdom test environment, creating artificial gaps in otherwise well-covered code.
